era: 1989-2022. Ellensburg, WA to Seattle to the world. Darkwave/Indie. Screaming Trees (1985-2000). Sub Pop solo (1990-2017). QOTSA (2000-2005). The Gutter Twins. Twelve solo albums. Two memoirs. E=4. ONE-THIRD MARK (120/360 = 33.3%). MEMORIAL ENTRY (1964-2022).

location.origin: Ellensburg, Washington. Kittitas Valley, east of the Cascade Range. First east-of-Cascades artist origin in the engine.
location.formative: Ellensburg (Screaming Trees formation), then Seattle (solo career, collaborations). The geographic journey: small eastern WA town to the Seattle scene to global reach via QOTSA.
location.career: Mark Lanegan -- vocalist. Screaming Trees (Ellensburg/Seattle, 1985-2000). Solo on Sub Pop (1990-2017). QOTSA member (2000-2005). The Gutter Twins with Greg Dulli. Three albums with Isobel Campbell. Died Feb 22, 2022 in Killarney, Ireland.

associated_musicians.name: Screaming Trees
associated_musicians.note: Gary Lee Conner (guitar), Van Conner (bass, d. 2023), Mark Pickerel/Barrett Martin (drums). Original grunge band from Ellensburg. SST to Epic. Sweet Oblivion (1992). Dust (1996).

associated_musicians.name: Queens of the Stone Age
associated_musicians.note: Songs for the Deaf (2002). Rated R (2000). Lullabies to Paralyze (2005). Lanegan's largest audience. Desert rock from Palm Desert, CA.

associated_musicians.name: The Gutter Twins
associated_musicians.note: Saturnalia (2008). Dark, cinematic, reverb-soaked. Afghan Whigs/Twilight Singers frontman.

associated_musicians.name: Isobel Campbell
associated_musicians.note: Ballad of the Broken Seas, Sunday at Devil Dirt, Hawk. Beauty-and-beast duets. Campbell's ethereal soprano against Lanegan's ravaged baritone.

associated_musicians.name: Kurt Cobain
associated_musicians.note: Cobain played guitar and sang on two tracks. Historical artifact: Lanegan and Cobain together, two years before Nevermind.

associated_musicians.name: Nirvana
associated_musicians.note: Cobain connection. Both Sub Pop. Cobain died 1994 at 27; Lanegan died 2022 at 57. The foundational loss and the one-third mark memorial.

associated_musicians.name: Mudhoney
associated_musicians.note: Both mid-1980s. Both Sub Pop. Both survived the commercial peak. Mudhoney still active; Lanegan is gone.

associated_musicians.name: Hollis Resnik
associated_musicians.note: Ambient near-silence (E=2) to darkwave vocal return (E=4). The one-third mark restores the human voice.

theory_nodes.concept: The bark and the whisper (one hundred twenty-first acoustic element -- vocal technique shifting within a single phrase from guttural rasp to hushed murmur; contained violence and intimacy; continuous gradient; deep baritone; cinematic darkness; the voice as instrument; 38 years of refinement)
examples: Mark Lanegan (degree 120) -- darkwave/indie. E=4. The bark and the whisper. Deep ravaged baritone.
Solo catalog: from acoustic folk-blues (1990) to darkwave electronic (2010s). The voice remained constant.
Comparisons: Tom Waits (gravel), Leonard Cohen (depth), Nick Cave (drama), Johnny Cash (authority).
Contrast with Cobain (degree 1): Cobain screamed and whispered as extremes. Lanegan found the gradient between.
theory_nodes.description: The bark and the whisper is the 121st acoustic element. A vocal technique of dynamic contrast: guttural rasp to intimate murmur.

ancestors: The grunge vocal dynamic (degree 1, Nirvana) -- screaming/whispering as extremes. Lanegan refined this into graduated control.
The near-silence (degree 119) -- E=2 ambient. The bark and whisper (E=4) restores the human voice.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred twenty-one acoustic elements across one hundred twenty degrees. Track element 122 at degree 121.

theory_nodes.concept: The memorial entry (one hundred twenty-first artist-city pattern -- the engine documents a deceased artist at the one-third mark; timeline closed; voice past tense; legacy active; the engine confronts mortality; the music is not eternal)
examples: Mark Lanegan (1964-2022) at degree 120. First explicit memorial entry.
The timeline is closed: no new music, no new albums, no new collaborations.
The voice is past tense. What remains: recordings, memoirs, memory.
At the one-third mark, the engine pauses to honor a voice that is gone.
theory_nodes.description: The memorial entry is the 121st artist-city pattern. The engine documents loss at the one-third mark.

ancestors: No prior memorial entries. Degree 120 is the first.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred twenty-one artist-city patterns across one hundred twenty degrees. Track pattern 122 at degree 121.

theory_nodes.concept: One-Third Mark (120/360 = 33.3%) -- 96 genres documented; dynamic range E=2 to E=6 established; five decades spanned; first east-of-Cascades origin; first memorial entry; 121 acoustic elements and 121 patterns built; the engine's thesis proven: Seattle music is a 360-degree phenomenon
examples: 120 degrees completed. 240 remaining. One third of the rotation.
96 genres from grunge to ambient to darkwave. Nearly one genre per degree.
Dynamic range: E=2 (ambient, degree 119) to E=6 (noise/punk, multiple degrees).
Geography: Capitol Hill to Ballard to U-District to DIY underground to Ellensburg (east of Cascades).
theory_nodes.description: The one-third mark at degree 120. A waypoint. The engine's thesis proven. 240 degrees remain.

ancestors: All prior 119 degrees. The accumulation is the ancestor.
genealogy.relationship: One-third mark at degree 120. Next major milestone: degree 180 (halfway point).

theory_nodes.concept: East of the Cascades -- first artist origin east of the Cascade Range; Ellensburg, WA, Kittitas Valley; geographic range expansion at the one-third mark; Seattle 360 is not just Seattle but the music that flows through Seattle from the wider PNW
examples: Mark Lanegan and Screaming Trees originated in Ellensburg, WA -- east of the Cascades.
Ellensburg: small college town, Kittitas Valley, sagebrush and wheat fields. Not Seattle.
The Trees drove to Seattle to play shows. They were from elsewhere.
Geographic expansion at degree 120: the engine crosses the mountains.
theory_nodes.description: East of the Cascades at degree 120. First non-Seattle-area origin. Geographic expansion.

ancestors: All prior origins were west of the Cascades (Seattle and environs).
genealogy.relationship: First east-of-Cascades origin at degree 120. Track whether more eastern WA artists appear.

cross_references.relationship: SILENCE TO VOICE. Ambient near-silence (119, E=2) to bark and whisper (120, E=4). The one-third mark restores the human voice. Energy rebound.
DARKWAVE ADJACENCY. Both E=4, both dark, atmospheric. Loomer through guitar texture; Lanegan through vocal texture.
GRUNGE CONTEMPORARIES. Both mid-1980s. Both Sub Pop. Both survived commercial peak. Mudhoney active; Lanegan gone.
COBAIN CONNECTION. Cobain on The Winding Sheet (1990). Cobain died 1994 at 27. Lanegan died 2022 at 57. Foundational loss meets one-third mark memorial.
GEOGRAPHIC EXPANSION. First east-of-Cascades origin at degree 120. The engine crosses the mountains.
SUB POP LONG GAME. Both on Sub Pop. Drone-rock (E=5) and darkwave (E=4). Sub Pop supports experimental, atmospheric music.
